title: RAPO: Expanding Exploration for LLM Agents via Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization
authors: Siwei Zhang, Yun Xiong, Xi Chen, Zi'an Jia, Renhong Huang, Jiarong Xu, Jiawei Zhang
abstract: Agentic Reinforcement Learning (Agentic RL) has shown remarkable potential in large language model-based (LLM) agents. These works can empower LLM agents to tackle complex tasks via multi-step, tool-integrated reasoning. However, an inherent limitation of existing Agentic RL methods is their reliance on a pure on-policy paradigm for exploration, restricting exploration to the agent's self-generated outputs and preventing the discovery of new reasoning perspectives for further improvement. While recent efforts incorporate auxiliary off-policy signals to enhance exploration, they typically utilize full off-policy trajectories for trajectory-level policy estimation, overlooking the necessity for the fine-grained, step-level exploratory dynamics within agentic rollout. In this paper, we revisit exploration in Agentic RL and propose Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization (RAPO), a novel RL framework that introduces retrieval to explicitly expand exploration during training. To achieve this, we decompose the Agentic RL training process into two phases: (i) Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout, and (ii) Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization. Specifically, we propose a Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout strategy, which allows the agents to continuously reason over the retrieved off-policy step-level traces. It dynamically extends the reasoning receptive field of agents, enabling broader exploration conditioned on external behaviors. Subsequently, we introduce the Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization mechanism, which calibrates the policy gradient estimation with retrieval reward and importance shaping, stabilizing training and prioritizing retrieval-illuminating exploration. Extensive experiments show that RAPO achieves an +5.0% average gain on fourteen datasets across three agentic reasoning tasks, while delivering 1.2x faster training efficiency.
title_zh: RAPO：通过检索增强策略优化扩展大模型智能体的探索能力
abstract_zh:
基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在代理强化学习（Agentic Reinforcement Learning, Agentic RL）方面展现了巨大的潜力。这类方法能够使LLM智能体通过多步、工具融合的推理来应对复杂任务。然而，现有Agentic RL方法的一个固有局限在于其完全依赖于纯在线策略（on-policy）进行探索，导致探索仅限于智能体自身生成的输出，难以发现新的推理视角以进一步提升性能。尽管近期一些研究尝试引入辅助的离线策略（off-policy）信号以增强探索能力，但它们通常采用完整的离线轨迹来进行轨迹级策略估计，忽视了在代理推理过程中对细粒度、步骤级探索动态的需求。

本文重新审视了Agentic RL中的探索机制，提出了一种新颖的强化学习框架——检索增强型策略优化（Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization, RAPO），该框架通过引入检索机制，在训练过程中显式扩展探索范围。为实现这一目标，我们将Agentic RL的训练过程分解为两个阶段：(i) 混合策略代理推理（Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout），以及 (ii) 检索感知策略优化（Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization）。

具体而言，我们提出了一种混合策略代理推理策略，使智能体能够持续基于检索到的离线策略步骤级轨迹进行推理。该策略动态扩展了智能体的推理感知范围，使其能够基于外部行为进行更广泛的探索。随后，我们设计了检索感知策略优化机制，通过检索奖励与重要性调节（importance shaping）对策略梯度估计进行校准，从而稳定训练过程，并优先选择那些由检索所启发的探索路径。

大量实验表明，RAPO在三个代理推理任务的十四组数据集上实现了平均性能提升5.0%，同时训练效率提高了1.2倍。

title: SpecLoop: An Agentic RTL-to-Specification Framework with Formal Verification Feedback Loop
authors: Fu-Chieh Chang, Yu-Hsin Yang, Hung-Ming Huang, Yun-Chia Hsu, Yin-Yu Lin, Ming-Fang Tsai, Chun-Chih Yang, Pei-Yuan Wu
abstract: RTL implementations frequently lack up-to-date or consistent specifications, making comprehension, maintenance, and verification costly and error-prone. While prior work has explored generating specifications from RTL using large language models (LLMs), ensuring that the generated documents faithfully capture design intent remains a major challenge. We present SpecLoop, an agentic framework for RTL-to-specification generation with a formal-verification-driven iterative feedback loop. SpecLoop first generates candidate specifications and then reconstructs RTL from these specifications; it uses formal equivalence checking tools between the reconstructed RTL and the original design to validate functional consistency. When mismatches are detected, counterexamples are fed back to iteratively refine the specifications until equivalence is proven or no further progress can be made. Experiments across multiple LLMs and RTL benchmarks show that incorporating formal verification feedback substantially improves specification correctness and robustness over LLM-only baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of verification-guided specification generation.
title_zh: SpecLoop：一种具有形式化验证反馈环的智能RTL到规格框架
abstract_zh: RTL实现通常缺乏最新或一致的规格说明，导致理解、维护和验证成本高昂且容易出错。尽管先前的研究已探索利用大型语言模型（LLMs）从RTL生成规格说明，但确保生成的文档能准确反映设计意图仍是重大挑战。本文提出SpecLoop，一种基于形式化验证驱动的迭代反馈循环的代理式框架，用于RTL到规格说明的自动生成。SpecLoop首先生成候选规格说明，然后根据这些规格说明重构RTL；接着利用形式等价性检查工具对比重构后的RTL与原始设计，以验证功能一致性。当检测到不一致时，反例将被反馈用于迭代优化规格说明，直至证明等价性或无法进一步推进。在多个LLM和RTL基准上的实验表明，引入形式化验证反馈可显著提升规格说明的正确性和鲁棒性，相较于仅依赖LLM的基线方法，充分证明了验证引导式规格生成的有效性。

title: From Heuristic Selection to Automated Algorithm Design: LLMs Benefit from Strong Priors
authors: Qi Huang, Furong Ye, Ananta Shahane, Thomas Bäck, Niki van Stein
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have already been widely adopted for automated algorithm design, demonstrating strong abilities in generating and evolving algorithms across various fields. Existing work has largely focused on examining their effectiveness in solving specific problems, with search strategies primarily guided by adaptive prompt designs. In this paper, through investigating the token-wise attribution of the prompts to LLM-generated algorithmic codes, we show that providing high-quality algorithmic code examples can substantially improve the performance of the LLM-driven optimization. Building upon this insight, we propose leveraging prior benchmark algorithms to guide LLM-driven optimization and demonstrate superior performance on two black-box optimization benchmarks: the pseudo-Boolean optimization suite (pbo) and the black-box optimization suite (bbob). Our findings highlight the value of integrating benchmarking studies to enhance both efficiency and robustness of the LLM-driven black-box optimization methods.
title_zh: 从启发式选择到自动化算法设计：大语言模型得益于强大的先验知识
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）已在自动化算法设计中得到广泛应用，展现出在多个领域生成和演化算法的强大能力。现有研究主要集中在评估其解决特定问题的有效性，搜索策略通常依赖于自适应的提示设计。本文通过分析提示中每个标记（token）对LLM生成的算法代码的贡献度，发现提供高质量的算法代码示例能够显著提升LLM驱动优化的性能。基于这一发现，我们提出利用先前的基准算法来引导LLM驱动的优化，并在两个黑箱优化基准测试（伪布尔优化套件pbo和黑箱优化套件bbob）上验证了其优越性能。研究结果表明，将基准测试研究融入LLM驱动的黑箱优化方法，有助于显著提升其效率与鲁棒性。

title: Think, But Don't Overthink: Reproducing Recursive Language Models
authors: Daren Wang
abstract: This project reproduces and extends the recently proposed ``Recursive Language Models'' (RLMs) framework by Zhang et al. (2026). This framework enables Large Language Models (LLMs) to process near-infinite contexts by offloading the prompt into an external REPL environment. While the original paper relies on a default recursion depth of 1 and suggests deeper recursion as a future direction, this study specifically investigates the impact of scaling the recursion depth. Using state-of-the-art open-source agentic models (DeepSeek v3.2 and Kimi K2), I evaluated pure LLM, RLM (depth=1), and RLM (depth=2) on the S-NIAH and OOLONG benchmarks. The findings reveal a compelling phenomenon: Deeper recursion causes models to ``overthink''. While depth-1 RLMs effectively boost accuracy on complex reasoning tasks, applying deeper recursion (depth=2) or using RLMs on simple retrieval tasks paradoxically degrades performance and exponentially inflates execution time (e.g., from 3.6s to 344.5s) and token costs. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/drbillwang/rlm-reproduction
title_zh: 思考，但不要过度思考：复制递归语言模型
abstract_zh: 本项目复现并扩展了Zhang等人（2026）近期提出的“递归语言模型”（Recursive Language Models, RLMs）框架。该框架通过将提示（prompt）外置于外部REPL环境，使大型语言模型（LLMs）能够处理近乎无限长度的上下文。尽管原始论文默认采用递归深度为1，并将更深层次的递归视为未来研究方向，本研究则专门探讨了递归深度扩展的影响。我们使用当前最先进的开源代理型模型（DeepSeek v3.2 和 Kimi K2），在S-NIAH和OOLONG基准测试中评估了纯LLM、RLM（深度=1）以及RLM（深度=2）的表现。研究结果揭示了一个引人深思的现象：更深的递归会导致模型“过度思考”。虽然深度为1的RLM在复杂推理任务中显著提升了准确率，但采用更深递归（深度=2）或在简单检索任务中使用RLM反而导致性能下降，并使执行时间（例如从3.6秒激增至344.5秒）和token成本呈指数级增长。代码与数据已公开：https://github.com/drbillwang/rlm-reproduction

title: LiveAgentBench: Comprehensive Benchmarking of Agentic Systems Across 104 Real-World Challenges
authors: Hao Li, Huan Wang, Jinjie Gu, Wenjie Wang, Chenyi Zhuang, Sikang Bian
abstract: As large language models grow more capable, general AI agents have become increasingly prevalent in practical applications. However, existing benchmarks face significant limitations, failing to represent real-world user tasks accurately. To address this gap, we present LiveAgentBench, a comprehensive benchmark with 104 scenarios that reflect real user requirements. It is constructed from publicly sourced questions on social media and real-world products. Central to our approach is the Social Perception-Driven Data Generation (SPDG) method, a novel process we developed to ensure each question's real-world relevance, task complexity, and result verifiability. We evaluate various models, frameworks, and commercial products using LiveAgentBench, revealing their practical performance and identifying areas for improvement. This release includes 374 tasks, with 125 for validation and 249 for testing. The SPDG process enables continuous updates with fresh queries from real-world interactions.
title_zh: LiveAgentBench：跨104个真实世界挑战的智能体系统综合基准测试
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型能力的不断提升，通用人工智能代理在实际应用中日益普及。然而，现有的评估基准存在显著局限，难以准确反映真实用户任务。为弥补这一差距，我们提出了LiveAgentBench，这是一个涵盖104个场景的综合性评估基准，真实反映了用户实际需求。该基准的数据来源于社交媒体上的公开问题以及真实世界的产品使用场景。我们提出的核心方法是“社会感知驱动的数据生成”（Social Perception-Driven Data Generation, SPDG），这是一种创新的数据生成流程，旨在确保每个问题具备现实相关性、任务复杂性以及结果可验证性。我们利用LiveAgentBench对多种模型、框架及商业产品进行了评估，揭示了它们在实际应用中的表现，并识别出改进方向。本次发布包含374项任务，其中125项用于验证，249项用于测试。SPDG流程支持持续更新，能够引入来自真实交互的最新问题，保持基准的时效性与实用性。

title: BeyondSWE: Can Current Code Agent Survive Beyond Single-Repo Bug Fixing?
authors: Guoxin Chen, Fanzhe Meng, Jiale Zhao, Minghao Li, Daixuan Cheng, Huatong Song, Jie Chen, Yuzhi Lin, Hui Chen, Xin Zhao, Ruihua Song, Chang Liu, Cheng Chen, Kai Jia, Ji-Rong Wen
abstract: Current benchmarks for code agents primarily assess narrow, repository-specific fixes, overlooking critical real-world challenges such as cross-repository reasoning, domain-specialized problem solving, dependency-driven migration, and full-repository generation. To address this gap, we introduce BeyondSWE, a comprehensive benchmark that broadens existing evaluations along two axes - resolution scope and knowledge scope - using 500 real-world instances across four distinct settings. Experimental results reveal a significant capability gap: even frontier models plateau below 45% success, and no single model performs consistently across task types. To systematically investigate the role of external knowledge, we develop SearchSWE, a framework that integrates deep search with coding abilities. Our experiments show that search augmentation yields inconsistent gains and can in some cases degrade performance, highlighting the difficulty of emulating developer-like workflows that interleave search and reasoning during coding tasks. This work offers both a realistic, challenging evaluation benchmark and a flexible framework to advance research toward more capable code agents.
title_zh: BeyondSWE：当前的代码智能体能否超越单仓库缺陷修复？
abstract_zh: 当前代码智能体的基准测试主要聚焦于狭窄的、针对特定代码库的修复任务，忽视了现实世界中一些关键挑战，例如跨代码库推理、领域专业化问题求解、依赖驱动的迁移以及完整代码库的生成。为弥补这一差距，我们提出了BeyondSWE，一个全面的基准测试，通过在四个不同场景下涵盖500个真实世界实例，从“解决范围”和“知识范围”两个维度扩展了现有评估体系。实验结果揭示了显著的能力差距：即使是最先进的模型，成功率也未能突破45%，且没有任何单一模型能在所有任务类型上保持一致的优异表现。为系统研究外部知识的作用，我们开发了SearchSWE框架，该框架将深度搜索能力与编程能力相结合。实验表明，搜索增强带来的收益并不稳定，甚至在某些情况下会降低性能，凸显了在编码任务中模拟开发者“搜索与推理交替进行”的工作流程的难度。本研究不仅提供了一个真实且具有挑战性的评估基准，还构建了一个灵活的框架，推动代码智能体研究向更强大的方向发展。

title: Type-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Dependency Closure for Solver-Executable Industrial Optimization Modeling
authors: Y. Zhong, R. Huang, M. Wang, Z. Guo, YC. Li, M. Yu, Z. Jin
abstract: Automated industrial optimization modeling requires reliable translation of natural-language requirements into solver-executable code. However, large language models often generate non-compilable models due to missing declarations, type inconsistencies, and incomplete dependency contexts. We propose a type-aware retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) method that enforces modeling entity types and minimal dependency closure to ensure executability. Unlike existing RAG approaches that index unstructured text, our method constructs a domain-specific typed knowledge base by parsing heterogeneous sources, such as academic papers and solver code, into typed units and encoding their mathematical dependencies in a knowledge graph. Given a natural-language instruction, it performs hybrid retrieval and computes a minimal dependency-closed context, the smallest set of typed symbols required for solver-executable code, via dependency propagation over the graph. We validate the method on two constraint-intensive industrial cases: demand response optimization in battery production and flexible job shop scheduling. In the first case, our method generates an executable model incorporating demand-response incentives and load-reduction constraints, achieving peak shaving while preserving profitability; conventional RAG baselines fail. In the second case, it consistently produces compilable models that reach known optimal solutions, demonstrating robust cross-domain generalization; baselines fail entirely. Ablation studies confirm that enforcing type-aware dependency closure is essential for avoiding structural hallucinations and ensuring executability, addressing a critical barrier to deploying large language models in complex engineering optimization tasks.
title_zh: 基于依赖闭包的类型感知检索增强生成用于求解器可执行的工业优化建模
abstract_zh: 自动化工业优化建模需要将自然语言需求可靠地转化为可执行的求解器代码。然而，大型语言模型常因缺少声明、类型不一致以及依赖关系上下文不完整，生成无法编译的模型。为此，我们提出一种类型感知的检索增强生成（RAG）方法，通过强制实施建模实体类型和最小依赖闭包，确保模型的可执行性。与现有RAG方法索引非结构化文本不同，我们的方法通过解析异构来源（如学术论文和求解器代码）构建领域特定的类型化知识库，将这些来源转化为类型化单元，并在知识图谱中编码其数学依赖关系。给定一条自然语言指令，该方法执行混合检索，并通过图上的依赖传播计算出最小依赖闭包上下文——即生成可执行求解器代码所需的最小类型化符号集合。我们在两个约束密集型工业案例中验证了该方法的有效性：电池生产中的需求响应优化和柔性作业车间调度。在第一个案例中，我们的方法生成了包含需求响应激励和负荷削减约束的可执行模型，在保持盈利能力的同时实现削峰；而传统RAG基线方法则完全失败。在第二个案例中，该方法始终生成可编译的模型，并达到已知最优解，展现出强大的跨领域泛化能力；而基线方法则完全失效。消融实验表明，强制执行类型感知的依赖闭包对于避免结构幻觉、确保可执行性至关重要，有效解决了大型语言模型在复杂工程优化任务中部署的关键障碍。

title: Saarthi for AGI: Towards Domain-Specific General Intelligence for Formal Verification
authors: Aman Kumar, Deepak Narayan Gadde, Luu Danh Minh, Vaisakh Naduvodi Viswambharan, Keerthan Kopparam Radhakrishna, Sivaram Pothireddypalli
abstract: Saarthi is an agentic AI framework that uses multi-agent collaboration to perform end-to-end formal verification. Even though the framework provides a complete flow from specification to coverage closure, with around 40% efficacy, there are several challenges that need to be addressed to make it more robust and reliable. Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is still a distant goal, and current Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents are prone to hallucinations and making mistakes, especially when dealing with complex tasks such as formal verification. However, with the right enhancements and improvements, we believe that Saarthi can be a significant step towards achieving domain-specific general intelligence for formal verification. Especially for problems that require Short Term, Short Context (STSC) capabilities, such as formal verification, Saarthi can be a powerful tool to assist verification engineers in their work. In this paper, we present two key enhancements to the Saarthi framework: (1) a structured rulebook and specification grammar to improve the accuracy and controllability of SystemVerilog Assertion (SVA) generation, and (2) integration of advanced Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques, such as GraphRAG, to provide agents with access to technical knowledge and best practices for iterative refinement and improvement of outputs. We also benchmark these enhancements for the overall Saarthi framework using challenging test cases from NVIDIA's CVDP benchmark targeting formal verification. Our benchmark results stand out with a 70% improvement in the accuracy of generated assertions, and a 50% reduction in the number of iterations required to achieve coverage closure.
title_zh: Saarthi 用于 AGI：迈向形式化验证领域的专用通用智能
abstract_zh:
Saarthi 是一个基于多智能体协作的代理型 AI 框架，能够实现从规格说明到覆盖率闭合的端到端形式化验证。尽管该框架已提供从规格定义到覆盖率闭合的完整流程，且整体有效性约为 40%，但仍存在若干挑战亟待解决，以提升其鲁棒性与可靠性。目前，通用人工智能（AGI）仍是一个遥远的目标，而基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在处理复杂任务（如形式化验证）时，容易产生幻觉并犯错。然而，通过适当的增强与改进，我们相信 Saarthi 有望成为迈向形式化验证领域专用通用智能的重要一步。尤其对于需要短期、小上下文（Short Term, Short Context, STSC）能力的问题，如形式化验证，Saarthi 可成为辅助验证工程师工作的强大工具。

本文提出了对 Saarthi 框架的两项关键改进：（1）引入结构化的规则手册与规格语法，以提升 SystemVerilog 断言（SVA）生成的准确性与可控性；（2）集成先进的检索增强生成（Retrieval Augmented Generation, RAG）技术，如 GraphRAG，使智能体能够访问技术知识库与最佳实践，从而实现输出的迭代优化与持续改进。我们还基于 NVIDIA 的 CVDP 基准中针对形式化验证设计的高难度测试用例，对这些增强功能在 Saarthi 框架整体性能上的表现进行了基准测试。实验结果表明，生成断言的准确率提升了 70%，同时达到覆盖率闭合所需的迭代次数减少了 50%。

title: Agentic AI-based Coverage Closure for Formal Verification
authors: Sivaram Pothireddypalli, Ashish Raman, Deepak Narayan Gadde, Aman Kumar
abstract: Coverage closure is a critical requirement in Integrated Chip (IC) development process and key metric for verification sign-off. However, traditional exhaustive approaches often fail to achieve full coverage within project timelines. This study presents an agentic AI-driven workflow that utilizes Large Language Model (LLM)-enabled Generative AI (GenAI) to automate coverage analysis for formal verification, identify coverage gaps, and generate the required formal properties. The framework accelerates verification efficiency by systematically addressing coverage holes. Benchmarking open-source and internal designs reveals a measurable increase in coverage metrics, with improvements correlated to the complexity of the design. Comparative analysis validates the effectiveness of this approach. These results highlight the potential of agentic AI-based techniques to improve formal verification productivity and support comprehensive coverage closure.
title_zh: 基于代理型AI的正式验证覆盖率闭环
abstract_zh: 覆盖率闭合是集成电路（IC）开发过程中的关键要求，也是验证签收的重要指标。然而，传统的穷举式方法往往无法在项目周期内实现完全覆盖。本研究提出了一种基于智能体AI的自动化工作流，利用大语言模型（LLM）驱动的生成式人工智能（GenAI），实现形式化验证中的覆盖率分析，识别覆盖率缺口，并自动生成所需的形式化属性。该框架通过系统性地填补覆盖率漏洞，显著提升了验证效率。对开源及内部设计的基准测试表明，覆盖率指标得到明显提升，且改进程度与设计复杂度呈正相关。对比分析进一步验证了该方法的有效性。研究结果表明，基于智能体AI的技术具有显著提升形式化验证生产力的潜力，能够有效支持全面的覆盖率闭合。

title: A Practical Guide for Establishing a Technical Debt Management Process (Preprint)
authors: Marion Wiese, Kamila Serwa, Eva Bittner
abstract: Context. Technical Debt (TD) refers to short-term beneficial software solutions that impede future changes, making TD management essential. However, establishing a TD management (TDM) process is one of the most pressing concerns in practice. Goal. We plan to identify which previously researched TDM approaches are feasible in practice and what typical challenges emerge to create a guideline for establishing a TDM process. Method. We replicated our previously published action research study by conducting five workshops introducing TDM with two teams from different companies. To determine the feasibility of TDM approaches, we presented the teams with approaches for various TD activities and let them decide which to adopt. Overall, we conducted 19 workshops and retrospectives, analyzing 108 meetings (96 hours) over a 30-month period. Results. The adopted TD prevention strategies and documentation were similar in all teams. The teams utilized their respective backlogs and created a new backlog item type for TD, incorporating similar attributes such as interest, contagiousness, a resubmission date, and reminders to discuss drawbacks and risks. However, they used different prioritization approaches and deviating repayment methods. The teams had to overcome similar challenges during the establishment, which we list in this paper. Conclusions. We identified the TDM approaches used by all teams as a starting point for best practices. For challenges, we provided solutions or identified them as research gaps. Issue tracking system vendors should implement TD issue types employing the identified attributes. Finally, we created a white paper for practitioners to establish a TDM process based on our results.
title_zh: 建立技术债务管理流程的实用指南（预印本）
abstract_zh:
背景：技术债（Technical Debt, TD）指那些短期内有益但阻碍未来变更的软件解决方案，因此技术债管理（TDM）至关重要。然而，在实践中建立有效的TDM流程是当前最紧迫的挑战之一。  
目标：本研究旨在识别以往研究中哪些TDM方法在实际中可行，并总结实践中常见的挑战，从而为建立TDM流程提供指导性建议。  
方法：我们复现了之前发表的行动研究，通过组织五次工作坊，向来自两家不同公司的两个团队介绍TDM理念。为评估TDM方法的可行性，我们向团队展示了多种针对技术债活动的解决方案，由团队自主选择采纳。整个研究历时30个月，共开展19次工作坊与回顾会议，分析了108场会议（总计96小时）。  
结果：所有团队采纳的技术债预防策略和文档方法高度相似。各团队均利用自身现有的待办事项列表（backlog），并新增了一种“技术债”类型的待办事项，其属性包括：利益相关性、传染性、重新提交日期以及提醒机制，用于讨论潜在缺陷与风险。然而，各团队在优先级排序方法和偿还策略上存在差异。在实施过程中，各团队面临了相似的挑战，本文对此进行了归纳总结。  
结论：我们识别出所有团队均采用的TDM方法，作为最佳实践的起点。针对所遇到的挑战，我们提出了相应的解决方案，或指出了尚待研究的空白领域。建议问题跟踪系统供应商在实现技术债问题类型时，应采用本文识别出的关键属性。最后，基于研究结果，我们编制了一份面向实践者的白皮书，为建立TDM流程提供实用指导。

title: REGAL: A Registry-Driven Architecture for Deterministic Grounding of Agentic AI in Enterprise Telemetry
authors: Yuvraj Agrawal
abstract: Enterprise engineering organizations produce high-volume, heterogeneous telemetry from version control systems, CI/CD pipelines, issue trackers, and observability platforms. Large Language Models (LLMs) enable new forms of agentic automation, but grounding such agents on private telemetry raises three practical challenges: limited model context, locally defined semantic concepts, and evolving metric interfaces. We present REGAL, a registry-driven architecture for deterministic grounding of agentic AI systems in enterprise telemetry. REGAL adopts an explicitly architectural approach: deterministic telemetry computation is treated as a first-class primitive, and LLMs operate over a bounded, version-controlled action space rather than raw event streams. The architecture combines (1) a Medallion ELT pipeline that produces replayable, semantically compressed Gold artifacts, and (2) a registry-driven compilation layer that synthesizes Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools from declarative metric definitions. The registry functions as an "interface-as-code" layer, ensuring alignment between tool specification and execution, mitigating tool drift, and embedding governance policies directly at the semantic boundary. A prototype implementation and case study validate the feasibility of deterministic grounding and illustrate its implications for latency, token efficiency, and operational governance. This work systematizes an architectural pattern for enterprise LLM grounding; it does not propose new learning algorithms, but rather elevates deterministic computation and semantic compilation to first-class design primitives for agentic systems.
title_zh: REGAL：一种基于注册表的架构，用于在企业遥测中实现智能体AI的确定性定位
abstract_zh: 企业工程组织从版本控制系统、CI/CD流水线、问题追踪系统以及可观测性平台等来源产生大量异构的遥测数据。大型语言模型（LLMs）为新型代理自动化提供了可能，但将此类代理基于私有遥测数据进行可靠落地，面临三大实际挑战：模型上下文有限、本地定义的语义概念不一致，以及指标接口持续演进。本文提出REGAL——一种基于注册表驱动的架构，用于在企业遥测数据中实现代理型AI系统的确定性落地。REGAL采用显式架构设计：将确定性的遥测计算视为第一类原语，LLM不再直接处理原始事件流，而是在一个有界且版本可控的操作空间中运行。该架构包含两个核心组件：（1）Medallion ELT流水线，用于生成可重放、语义压缩的“黄金”数据资产；（2）基于注册表的编译层，能够从声明式指标定义自动生成模型上下文协议（MCP）工具。注册表作为“接口即代码”层，确保工具定义与执行的一致性，有效缓解工具漂移问题，并将治理策略直接嵌入语义边界。原型实现与案例研究验证了确定性落地的可行性，并展示了其在延迟、令牌效率和运营治理方面的实际影响。本工作系统化地提炼出企业级LLM落地的架构模式；它不涉及新的学习算法，而是将确定性计算与语义编译提升为代理系统设计中的第一类核心原语。

title: Speech recognition assisted by large language models to command software orally -- Application to an augmented and virtual reality web app for immersive molecular graphics
authors: Fabio Cortes Rodriguez, Luciano Abriata
abstract: This project successfully developed, evaluated and integrated a Voice User Interface (VUI) into a web application that we are developing for immersive molecular graphics. Said app provides augmented and virtual reality (AR and VR) environments where users manipulate molecules with their hands, but this means the hands can't be used to control the app through a regular mouse- and keyboard-based GUI. The speech-based VUI system developed here alleviates this problem, making it easy to control the app via natural spoken (or typed) commands. To achieve this VUI we evaluated two distinct Automated Speech Recognition (ASR) systems: Chrome's native Speech API and OpenAI's Whisper v3. While Whisper offered broader browser compatibility, its tendency to "hallucinate" with specialized scientific jargon proved very problematic. Consequently, we selected Chrome's ASR for its stability, speed, and reliability. For translating transcribed speech into software commands, we tested two Large Language Model (LLM)-driven approaches: either generating executable code, or calling predefined functions. The function call method, powered by OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini, was ultimately adopted due to its superior safety, efficiency, and reliability over the more complex and error-prone code-generation approach. The resulting VUI is then based on an integration of Chrome's ASR with our LLM-based function-calling module, enabling users to command the application using natural language as shown in a video linked inside this report. We provide links to live examples demonstrating all the intermediate components, and details on how we crafted the LLM's prompt in order to teach it the function calls as well as ways to clean up the transcribed speech and to explain itself while generating function calls. For best demonstration of the final system, we provide a video example.
title_zh: 借助大语言模型的语音识别技术实现软件的语音指令控制——应用于增强现实与虚拟现实网络应用中的沉浸式分子图形展示
abstract_zh:
本项目成功开发、评估并集成了一套语音用户界面（VUI），将其应用于我们正在开发的沉浸式分子图形网络应用中。该应用提供增强现实（AR）和虚拟现实（VR）环境，用户可通过手势直接操控分子，但这也意味着双手无法同时用于传统基于鼠标和键盘的图形用户界面（GUI）操作。本项目所开发的基于语音的VUI系统有效解决了这一问题，使用户能够通过自然语言（口语或输入文字）轻松控制应用程序。

为实现该VUI，我们评估了两种不同的自动语音识别（ASR）系统：Chrome原生语音API与OpenAI的Whisper v3。尽管Whisper在浏览器兼容性方面表现更广，但其在处理专业科学术语时容易“幻觉”（即产生不准确或虚构的识别结果），这带来了严重问题。因此，我们最终选择使用Chrome的ASR，因其在稳定性、速度和可靠性方面表现更优。

在将识别出的语音转化为软件指令方面，我们测试了两种基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法：一是生成可执行代码，二是调用预定义函数。最终，我们采纳了函数调用方法，该方法由OpenAI的GPT-4o-mini驱动，相比更复杂且易出错的代码生成方式，其在安全性、效率和可靠性方面均表现更佳。

最终的VUI系统整合了Chrome的ASR与我们基于LLM的函数调用模块，使用户能够通过自然语言指令控制应用程序。相关功能演示视频已附在本报告内。我们还提供了所有中间组件的在线演示链接，并详细说明了如何设计LLM提示词（prompt），以教会模型正确执行函数调用；同时介绍了对语音识别结果进行清理的方法，以及模型在生成函数调用时的自我解释机制。为最佳展示最终系统效果，我们特别提供了一段视频示例。

title: His2Trans: A Skeleton First Framework for Self Evolving C to Rust Translation with Historical Retrieval
authors: Shengbo Wang, Mingwei Liu, Guangsheng Ou, Yuwen Chen, Zike Li, Yanlin Wang, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Automated C-to-Rust migration encounters systemic obstacles when scaling from code snippets to industrial projects, mainly because build context is often unavailable ("dependency hell") and domain-specific evolutionary knowledge is missing. As a result, current LLM-based methods frequently cannot reconstruct precise type definitions under complex build systems or infer idiomatic API correspondences, which in turn leads to hallucinated dependencies and unproductive repair loops. To tackle these issues, we introduce His2Trans, a framework that combines a deterministic, build-aware skeleton with self-evolving knowledge extraction to support stable, incremental migration. On the structural side, His2Trans performs build tracing to create a compilable Project-Level Skeleton Graph, providing a strictly typed environment that separates global verification from local logic generation. On the cognitive side, it derives fine-grained API and code-fragment rules from historical migration traces and uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system to steer the LLM toward idiomatic interface reuse. Experiments on industrial OpenHarmony modules show that His2Trans reaches a 99.75% incremental compilation pass rate, effectively fixing build failures where baselines struggle. On general-purpose benchmarks, it lowers the unsafe code ratio by 23.6 percentage points compared to C2Rust while producing the fewest warnings. Finally, knowledge accumulation studies demonstrate the framework's evolutionary behavior: by continuously integrating verified patterns, His2Trans cuts repair overhead on unseen tasks by about 60%.
title_zh: His2Trans：一种基于骨架优先的自演化C到Rust翻译框架及其历史检索机制
abstract_zh:
自动化C语言到Rust语言的迁移在从代码片段扩展到工业级项目时面临系统性障碍，主要原因在于构建环境通常不可用（“依赖地狱”）以及缺乏领域特定的演化知识。因此，当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法在复杂构建系统下难以重建精确的类型定义，也无法准确推断出符合惯例的API对应关系，从而导致生成虚假依赖项并陷入无意义的修复循环。为解决这些问题，我们提出His2Trans框架，该框架结合确定性的、构建感知的骨架结构与自我演化的知识提取机制，支持稳定且增量式的迁移过程。

在结构层面，His2Trans通过构建追踪（build tracing）生成可编译的“项目级骨架图”（Project-Level Skeleton Graph），构建一个严格类型化的环境，将全局验证与局部逻辑生成分离，确保迁移过程的稳定性。在认知层面，该框架从历史迁移记录中提取细粒度的API与代码片段规则，并利用检索增强生成（Retrieval-Augmented Generation, RAG）系统引导LLM实现符合惯例的接口复用。

在工业级OpenHarmony模块上的实验表明，His2Trans实现了99.75%的增量编译通过率，有效解决了基线方法难以应对的构建失败问题。在通用基准测试中，其相比C2Rust将不安全代码比例降低了23.6个百分点，同时生成的警告数量最少。最后，知识积累研究进一步验证了框架的演化能力：通过持续整合经过验证的迁移模式，His2Trans在面对未见过的任务时，将修复开销降低了约60%。

title: SUN: Shared Use of Next-token Prediction for Efficient Multi-LLM Disaggregated Serving
authors: Sunghyeon Woo, Ahreum Seo, Jaegwang Lee, Jaeeun Kil, Hanbae Seo, Joonghoon Kim, Baeseong Park, Se Jung Kwon, Dongsoo Lee
abstract: In multi-model LLM serving, decode execution remains inefficient due to model-specific resource partitioning: since cross-model batching is not possible, memory-bound decoding often suffers from severe GPU underutilization, especially under skewed workloads. We propose Shared Use of Next-token Prediction (SUN), the first approach that enables cross-model sharing of decode execution in disaggregated multi-LLM serving. SUN decomposes a decoder-only Transformer into a prefill module and a decode module, and fine-tunes only the task-specific prefill module, enabling a frozen decode module to be shared across models. This design enables a model-agnostic decode routing policy that balances decode requests across shared workers to maximize utilization. Across diverse tasks and model families, SUN achieves accuracy comparable to full fine-tuning while maintaining system throughput with fewer decode workers. In particular, SUN improves throughput per GPU by up to 2.0x over conventional disaggregation while keeping time-per-output-token (TPOT) within 5%. SUN inherently enables and facilitates low-bit decoding; with Quantized SUN (QSUN), it achieves a 45% speedup with comparable accuracy to SUN while preserving the benefits of shared decoding.
title_zh: SUN：用于高效多大语言模型解耦服务的下一词预测共享机制
abstract_zh: 在多模型大语言模型（LLM）服务中，解码执行效率低下，主要原因是模型特定的资源划分：由于跨模型批处理不可行，内存受限的解码过程常常导致GPU利用率严重不足，尤其是在工作负载分布不均的情况下。我们提出了共享下一词预测（Shared Use of Next-token Prediction, SUN），这是首个在解耦式多LLM服务中实现跨模型解码执行共享的方法。SUN将仅解码器的Transformer模型分解为预填充模块和解码模块，仅对任务特定的预填充模块进行微调，从而使得冻结的解码模块可在多个模型间共享。这一设计支持与模型无关的解码路由策略，能够将解码请求均衡分配到共享的工作节点上，以最大化资源利用率。在多种任务和模型家族中，SUN在保持系统吞吐量的同时，实现了与全量微调相当的精度，且所需解码工作节点更少。特别地，SUN在保持每输出token耗时（TPOT）仅增加5%的前提下，相比传统解耦架构，实现了每GPU吞吐量最高达2.0倍的提升。SUN天然支持并促进了低比特解码；通过量化版本的SUN（QSUN），在保持共享解码优势的同时，实现了45%的加速，且精度与SUN相当。

title: Pecker: Bug Localization Framework for Sequential Designs via Causal Chain Reconstruction
authors: Jiaping Tang, Jianan Mu, Tianyun Ma, Zhiteng Chao, Jing Ye, Huawei Li
abstract: Debugging represents a time-consuming and labor-intensive task in hardware design, with bug localization constituting a substantial portion of this process. While spectrum-based bug localization techniques have achieved remarkable success in software domains and shown promise for hardware description languages, their effectiveness severely degrades in sequential designs. Unlike software programs, hardware designs exhibit intrinsic temporal characteristics that create fundamental challenges: timing misalignment between bug activation and observation, and progressive error propagation through state elements that obscures the root cause. To address these limitations, we propose Pecker, a novel bug localization framework that reconstructs the broken causal chain in sequential designs. Our approach introduces two key innovations: temporal backtracking using Estimated Minimal Propagation Cycles to identify potential activation cycles, strategic trace pruning to eliminate state pollution effects. We evaluate Pecker on comprehensive benchmarks comprising both combinational and sequential circuits. Experimental results demonstrate that Pecker effectively localizes 51%/80%/85% bugs within Top-1/3/5 ranks respectively, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art techniques. Notably, Pecker maintains robust performance across circuit complexities while existing methods exhibit severe degradation on sequential designs.
title_zh: Pecker：通过因果链重构实现顺序设计的缺陷定位框架
abstract_zh: 调试在硬件设计中是一项耗时且费力的任务，而定位错误又是该过程中的主要组成部分。尽管基于谱的错误定位技术在软件领域取得了显著成功，并在硬件描述语言中展现出潜力，但其在时序电路设计中的效果却严重下降。与软件程序不同，硬件设计具有固有的时序特性，这带来了根本性挑战：错误激活与观测之间存在时间错位，以及通过状态元件传播的错误逐步扩散，从而掩盖了根本原因。为解决这些局限性，我们提出了Pecker——一种新颖的错误定位框架，用于重建时序设计中被破坏的因果链。我们的方法引入了两项关键创新：利用“估计最小传播周期”进行时间回溯，以识别潜在的激活周期；通过策略性地裁剪追踪信息，消除状态污染的影响。我们在涵盖组合电路和时序电路的全面基准测试集上评估了Pecker。实验结果表明，Pecker能够将51%/80%/85%的错误定位在Top-1/3/5排名内，显著优于现有最先进方法。值得注意的是，Pecker在不同复杂度的电路中均保持了稳健的性能，而现有方法在时序设计上则表现出严重的性能退化。

title: TinyIceNet: Low-Power SAR Sea Ice Segmentation for On-Board FPGA Inference
authors: Mhd Rashed Al Koutayni, Mohamed Selim, Gerd Reis, Alain Pagani, Didier Stricker
abstract: Accurate sea ice mapping is essential for safe maritime navigation in polar regions, where rapidly changing ice conditions require timely and reliable information. While Sentinel-1 Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides high-resolution, all-weather observations of sea ice, conventional ground-based processing is limited by downlink bandwidth, latency, and energy costs associated with transmitting large volumes of raw data. On-board processing, enabled by dedicated inference chips integrated directly within the satellite payload, offers a transformative alternative by generating actionable sea ice products in orbit. In this context, we present TinyIceNet, a compact semantic segmentation network co-designed for on-board Stage of Development (SOD) mapping from dual-polarized Sentinel-1 SAR imagery under strict hardware and power constraints. Trained on the AI4Arctic dataset, TinyIceNet combines SAR-aware architectural simplifications with low-precision quantization to balance accuracy and efficiency. The model is synthesized using High-Level Synthesis and deployed on a Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ FPGA platform, demonstrating near-real-time inference with significantly reduced energy consumption. Experimental results show that TinyIceNet achieves 75.216% F1 score on SOD segmentation while reducing energy consumption by 2x compared to full-precision GPU baselines, underscoring the potential of chip-level hardware-algorithm co-design for future spaceborne and edge AI systems.
title_zh: TinyIceNet：面向机载FPGA推理的低功耗SAR海冰分割
abstract_zh: 精确的海冰制图对于极地地区安全的海上航行至关重要，因为快速变化的冰情需要及时可靠的资讯。尽管哨兵-1号合成孔径雷达（SAR）能够提供高分辨率、全天候的海冰观测数据，但传统的地面处理方式受限于下行链路带宽、延迟以及传输大量原始数据所导致的高昂能耗。通过在卫星载荷中集成专用推理芯片实现星上处理，为生成即时可用的海冰产品提供了变革性的解决方案。在此背景下，本文提出TinyIceNet——一种专为星上开发阶段（SOD）海冰制图而协同设计的轻量级语义分割网络，能够基于双极化哨兵-1号SAR影像，在严格的硬件与功耗约束下运行。TinyIceNet在AI4Arctic数据集上进行训练，结合了面向SAR数据的架构简化方法与低精度量化技术，在精度与效率之间取得良好平衡。该模型采用高层次综合（High-Level Synthesis）技术进行硬件实现，并部署于Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ FPGA平台，实现了接近实时的推理性能，同时显著降低了能耗。实验结果表明，TinyIceNet在SOD分类任务中达到75.216%的F1分数，相较于全精度GPU基线模型，能耗降低达2倍，充分展示了芯片级软硬件协同设计在未来的星载与边缘人工智能系统中的巨大潜力。

title: Design Generative AI for Practitioners: Exploring Interaction Approaches Aligned with Creative Practice
authors: Xiaohan Peng, Wendy E. Mackay, Janin Koch
abstract: Design is a non-linear, reflective process in which practitioners engage with visual, semantic, and other expressive materials to explore, iterate, and refine ideas. As Generative AI (GenAI) becomes integrated into professional design practice, traditional interaction approaches focusing on prompts or whole-image manipulation can misalign AI output with designers' intent, forcing visual thinkers into verbal reasoning or post-hoc adjustments. We present three interaction approaches from DesignPrompt, FusAIn, and DesignTrace that distribute control across intent, input, and process, enabling designers to guide AI alignment at different stages of interaction. We further argue that alignment is a dynamic negotiation, with AI adopting proactive or reactive roles according to designers' instrumental and inspirational needs and the creative stage.
title_zh: 为实践者设计生成式人工智能：探索与创意实践相契合的交互方式
abstract_zh: 设计是一种非线性、反思性的过程，设计师通过与视觉、语义及其他表达性材料的互动，不断探索、迭代并完善创意。随着生成式人工智能（GenAI）逐步融入专业设计实践，传统的以提示词或整体图像操作为中心的交互方式，往往难以与设计师的意图保持一致，迫使视觉思维者转向语言推理或事后调整。本文介绍了来自DesignPrompt、FusAIn和DesignTrace的三种交互方法，它们将控制权在意图、输入和过程之间进行分配，使设计师能够在交互的不同阶段引导AI的对齐。我们进一步提出，对齐是一个动态协商的过程，AI会根据设计师在不同创作阶段的工具性与启发性需求，主动或被动地扮演相应角色。

title: TikZilla: Scaling Text-to-TikZ with High-Quality Data and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Christian Greisinger, Steffen Eger
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to assist scientists across diverse workflows. A key challenge is generating high-quality figures from textual descriptions, often represented as TikZ programs that can be rendered as scientific images. Prior research has proposed a variety of datasets and modeling approaches for this task. However, existing datasets for Text-to-TikZ are too small and noisy to capture the complexity of TikZ, causing mismatches between text and rendered figures. Moreover, prior approaches rely solely on supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which does not expose the model to the rendered semantics of the figure, often resulting in errors such as looping, irrelevant content, and incorrect spatial relations. To address these issues, we construct DaTikZ-V4, a dataset more than four times larger and substantially higher in quality than DaTikZ-V3, enriched with LLM-generated figure descriptions. Using this dataset, we train TikZilla, a family of small open-source Qwen models (3B and 8B) with a two-stage pipeline of SFT followed by reinforcement learning (RL). For RL, we leverage an image encoder trained via inverse graphics to provide semantically faithful reward signals. Extensive human evaluations with over 1,000 judgments show that TikZilla improves by 1.5-2 points over its base models on a 5-point scale, surpasses GPT-4o by 0.5 points, and matches GPT-5 in the image-based evaluation, while operating at much smaller model sizes. Code, data, and models will be made available.
title_zh: TikZilla：基于高质量数据与强化学习的文本到TikZ缩放模型
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）正越来越多地用于协助科学家完成多样化的科研工作流程。其中一项关键挑战是从文本描述生成高质量的图表，这些图表通常以TikZ程序的形式表示，可被渲染为科学图像。此前的研究提出了多种数据集和建模方法来解决这一任务。然而，现有的Text-to-TikZ数据集规模过小且噪声较多，难以充分捕捉TikZ的复杂性，导致文本描述与最终渲染出的图像之间出现不匹配。此外，以往的方法仅依赖监督微调（SFT），未能让模型接触到图像的语义信息，常常导致循环错误、无关内容或空间关系错误等问题。

为解决上述问题，我们构建了DaTikZ-V4数据集，其规模超过DaTikZ-V3的四倍，且质量显著提升，其中还包含由大语言模型生成的图像描述。基于该数据集，我们训练了TikZilla——一系列小型开源Qwen模型（3B和8B），采用两阶段训练流程：先进行监督微调（SFT），再通过强化学习（RL）进一步优化。在强化学习阶段，我们利用通过逆向图形方法训练的图像编码器，提供语义忠实的奖励信号。大量人工评估（超过1000次判断）表明，TikZilla在5分制评分中比其基础模型提升1.5至2分，比GPT-4o高出0.5分，并在基于图像的评估中达到与GPT-5相当的水平，同时模型规模远小于后者。代码、数据和模型将全部公开发布。

title: Agentified Assessment of Logical Reasoning Agents
authors: Zhiyu Ni, Yifeng Xiao, Zheng Liang
abstract: We present a framework for evaluating and benchmarking logical reasoning agents when assessment itself must be reproducible, auditable, and robust to execution failures. Building on agentified assessment, we use an assessor agent to issue tasks, enforce execution budgets, parse outputs, and record structured failure types, while the agent under test only needs to expose a standardized agent-to-agent interface. As a case study, we benchmark an auto-formalization agent for first-order logic (FOL) reasoning on a solver-verified and repaired split of FOLIO. The agent translates natural language premises and conclusions into executable Z3Py programs and employs satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) solving to determine logical entailment. On the cleaned FOLIO validation set, the auto-formalization agent achieves 86.70% accuracy under the assessor protocol, outperforming a chain-of-thought baseline (73.89%).
title_zh: 逻辑推理代理的代理化评估
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种评估和基准测试逻辑推理智能体的框架，该框架在评估过程本身必须具备可复现性、可审计性，并且对执行失败具有鲁棒性的情况下依然有效。基于“代理化评估”的理念，我们使用一个评估代理来发布任务、执行预算控制、解析输出结果，并记录结构化的失败类型，而待测智能体仅需提供标准化的智能体间接口。作为案例研究，我们在一个经过求解器验证并修复的FOLIO数据集上，对一个用于一阶逻辑（FOL）推理的自动形式化智能体进行了基准测试。该智能体将自然语言的前提和结论转化为可执行的Z3Py程序，并利用可满足性模理论（SMT）求解来判断逻辑蕴含关系。在清理后的FOLIO验证集上，该自动形式化智能体在评估代理协议下达到了86.70%的准确率，显著优于链式思维基线方法（73.89%）。

title: Rethinking Code Similarity for Automated Algorithm Design with LLMs
authors: Rui Zhang, Zhichao Lu
abstract: The rise of Large Language Model-based Automated Algorithm Design (LLM-AAD) has transformed algorithm development by autonomously generating code implementations of expert-level algorithms. Unlike traditional expert-driven algorithm development, in the LLM-AAD paradigm, the main design principle behind an algorithm is often implicitly embedded in the generated code. Therefore, assessing algorithmic similarity directly from code, distinguishing genuine algorithmic innovation from mere syntactic variation, becomes essential. While various code similarity metrics exist, they fail to capture algorithmic similarity, as they focus on surface-level syntax or output equivalence rather than the underlying algorithmic logic. We propose BehaveSim, a novel method to measure algorithmic similarity through the lens of problem-solving behavior as a sequence of intermediate solutions produced during execution, dubbed as problem-solving trajectories (PSTrajs). By quantifying the alignment between PSTrajs using dynamic time warping (DTW), BehaveSim distinguishes algorithms with divergent logic despite syntactic or output-level similarities. We demonstrate its utility in two key applications: (i) Enhancing LLM-AAD: Integrating BehaveSim into existing LLM-AAD frameworks (e.g., FunSearch, EoH) promotes behavioral diversity, significantly improving performance on three AAD tasks. (ii) Algorithm analysis: BehaveSim clusters generated algorithms by behavior, enabling systematic analysis of problem-solving strategies--a crucial tool for the growing ecosystem of AI-generated algorithms. Data and code of this work are open-sourced at https://github.com/RayZhhh/behavesim.
title_zh: 利用大语言模型重新思考代码相似性在自动化算法设计中的应用
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型的自动化算法设计（LLM-AAD）的兴起，通过自主生成专家级算法的代码实现，彻底改变了算法开发的方式。与传统的专家驱动型算法开发不同，在LLM-AAD范式中，算法的核心设计思想往往隐含在生成的代码之中。因此，直接从代码层面评估算法相似性，区分真正的算法创新与仅在语法层面的变体，变得至关重要。尽管已存在多种代码相似性度量方法，但它们难以捕捉算法层面的相似性，因为这些方法通常只关注表面语法结构或输出结果的一致性，而忽略了底层的算法逻辑。为此，我们提出了BehaveSim——一种全新的算法相似性度量方法，其核心思想是通过问题求解行为来衡量算法相似性，具体表现为算法执行过程中产生的中间解序列，即问题求解轨迹（PSTrajs）。通过使用动态时间规整（DTW）技术量化PSTrajs之间的对齐程度，BehaveSim能够有效区分那些在语法或输出上看似相似但逻辑本质不同的算法。我们展示了该方法在两个关键应用场景中的有效性：（i）提升LLM-AAD性能：将BehaveSim集成到现有LLM-AAD框架（如FunSearch、EoH）中，可显著促进行为多样性，大幅提高在三个自动化算法设计任务上的表现；（ii）算法分析：BehaveSim可根据算法的行为进行聚类，从而实现对问题求解策略的系统性分析——这对于日益发展的AI生成算法生态体系而言，是一项至关重要的分析工具。本工作的数据与代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/RayZhhh/behavesim。

title: EvoSkill: Automated Skill Discovery for Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Salaheddin Alzubi, Noah Provenzano, Jaydon Bingham, Weiyuan Chen, Tu Vu
abstract: Coding agents are increasingly used as general-purpose problem solvers, but their flexibility does not by itself confer the domain expertise needed for specialized tasks. Recent work addresses this through \textit{agent skills}: reusable workflows, and code, that augment agents with domain-specific capabilities. Most skills today are hand-crafted, and existing evolutionary approaches optimize low-level artifacts (e.g. prompts \& code) that are tightly coupled to specific models and tasks. We introduce \textbf{EvoSkill}, a self-evolving framework that automatically discovers and refines agent skills through iterative failure analysis. EvoSkill analyzes execution failures, proposes new skills or edits to existing ones, and materializes them into structured, reusable skill folders. A Pareto frontier of agent programs governs selection, retaining only skills that improve held-out validation performance while the underlying model remains frozen. We evaluate EvoSkill on two benchmarks: OfficeQA, a grounded reasoning benchmark over U.S.\ Treasury data, where it improves exact-match accuracy by \textbf{7.3\%} (60.6\% $\to$ 67.9\%); and SealQA, a search-augmented QA benchmark with noisy retrieval, where it yields a \textbf{12.1\%} gain (26.6\% $\to$ 38.7\%). We also investigate the zero-shot transfer capabilties of skills evolved on one task to the other; in particular: skills evolved from SealQA transfers zero-shot to BrowseComp, improving accuracy by \textbf{5.3\%} without modification demonstrating that skill-level optimization produces transferable capabilities beyond the training task.
title_zh: EvoSkill：多智能体系统中的自动化技能发现
abstract_zh: 编码代理正被越来越多地用作通用问题求解器，但其灵活性本身并不能赋予完成专业任务所需的领域专长。近期研究通过引入“代理技能”（agent skills）来解决这一问题：即可复用的工作流和代码，用以增强代理在特定领域的专业能力。目前大多数技能都是人工设计的，而现有的进化方法仅优化与特定模型和任务紧密耦合的低级组件（如提示词和代码）。我们提出了**EvoSkill**——一种自我进化的框架，能够通过迭代的失败分析自动发现并优化代理技能。EvoSkill分析执行失败，提出新的技能或对现有技能进行修改，并将这些技能以结构化、可复用的技能文件夹形式落地。通过帕累托前沿（Pareto frontier）控制技能选择，仅保留那些在未见验证集上提升性能、同时底层模型保持冻结的技能。我们在两个基准测试上评估了EvoSkill：OfficeQA，一个基于美国财政部数据的具身推理基准，EvoSkill将精确匹配准确率提升了**7.3%**（从60.6%提升至67.9%）；SealQA，一个带有噪声检索的搜索增强型问答基准，EvoSkill带来了**12.1%**的性能提升（从26.6%提升至38.7%）。我们还研究了在某一任务上进化出的技能向另一任务的零样本迁移能力：特别是，从SealQA中进化出的技能可零样本迁移至BrowseComp任务，无需任何修改即提升准确率**5.3%**，这表明在技能层面进行优化能够产生超越训练任务本身的可迁移能力。

title: OrchMAS: Orchestrated Reasoning with Multi Collaborative Heterogeneous Scientific Expert Structured Agents
authors: Yichao Feng, Haoran Luo, Zhenghong Lin, Yiqun Sun, Pengfei Wei, Lawrence B. Hsieh, Anh Tuan Luu
abstract: Multi-agent large language model frameworks are promising for complex multi step reasoning, yet existing systems remain weak for scientific and knowledge intensive domains due to static prompts and agent roles, rigid workflows, and homogeneous model reliance, leading to poor domain adaptation, limited reasoning flexibility, and high latency on heterogeneous or long-horizon scientific tasks. They also struggle to revise earlier decisions when intermediate reasoning diverges, reducing reliability in structured and calculation heavy settings. To address these limitations, we propose a scientific domain oriented interactive two tier multi model orchestration framework. A dedicated orchestration model analyzes each task, dynamically constructs a domain aware reasoning pipeline, and instantiates specialized expert agents with tailored prompts, while an execution model performs each step under generated role and instruction specifications. The orchestrator iteratively updates the pipeline based on intermediate feedback, enabling dynamic replanning, role reallocation, and prompt refinement across multi turn interactions, strengthening robustness and specialization for scientific reasoning through structured heterogeneous model collaboration. The framework is model agnostic and supports heterogeneous LLM integration with different capacities or costs, enabling flexible performance efficiency trade offs in practical scientific deployments. Experiments show consistent improvements over existing multi agent systems and strong baselines across diverse reasoning and scientific style benchmarks.
title_zh: OrchMAS：基于多协同异构科学专家结构化智能体的协同推理
abstract_zh: 多智能体大语言模型框架在复杂多步骤推理任务中展现出巨大潜力，但现有系统在科学与知识密集型领域仍表现不佳，主要受限于静态提示词与固定智能体角色、僵化的流程设计以及对同质化模型的依赖，导致领域适应能力差、推理灵活性有限，并在异构或长周期科学任务中产生较高的延迟。此外，当中间推理过程出现偏差时，这些系统难以修正早期决策，从而降低了在结构化和计算密集型场景下的可靠性。为解决上述问题，我们提出了一种面向科学领域的交互式两级多模型协同框架。该框架通过专用的编排模型对任务进行分析，动态构建具备领域感知能力的推理流程，并根据需求实例化具有定制化提示词的专用专家智能体；执行模型则在生成的角色与指令规范下完成每一步操作。编排器通过持续接收中间反馈迭代更新推理流程，实现动态重规划、角色重新分配与提示词优化，支持多轮交互中的灵活调整，从而通过结构化的异构模型协作显著提升科学推理的鲁棒性与专业化水平。该框架具备模型无关性，支持集成具有不同能力或成本特性的异构大语言模型，可在实际科学应用中灵活权衡性能与效率。实验结果表明，该框架在多种推理与科学风格基准测试中均显著优于现有多智能体系统及强基线模型，展现出一致且显著的性能提升。

title: CoDAR: Continuous Diffusion Language Models are More Powerful Than You Think
authors: Junzhe Shen, Jieru Zhao, Ziwei He, Zhouhan Lin
abstract: We study why continuous diffusion language models (DLMs) have lagged behind discrete diffusion approaches despite their appealing continuous generative dynamics. Under a controlled token--recovery study, we identify token rounding, the final projection from denoised embeddings to tokens, as a primary bottleneck. Building on these insights, we propose CoDAR (Continuous Diffusion with Contextual AutoRegressive Decoder), a two--stage framework that keeps diffusion entirely continuous in an embedding space while learning a strong, context--conditional discretizer: an autoregressive Transformer decoder that cross--attends to the denoised embedding sequence and performs contextualized rounding to tokens. Experiments on LM1B and OpenWebText demonstrate that CoDAR substantially improves generation quality over latent diffusion and becomes competitive with strong discrete DLMs, while exposing a simple decoder--temperature knob to navigate the fluency--diversity trade off.
title_zh: CoDAR：连续扩散语言模型比你想象的更强大
abstract_zh: 我们研究了为何连续扩散语言模型（DLMs）尽管具有吸引人的连续生成动态，却在性能上落后于离散扩散方法。在一项受控的词元恢复实验中，我们识别出词元舍入（token rounding）——即从去噪嵌入到词元的最终映射过程——是主要瓶颈。基于这些发现，我们提出了CoDAR（上下文感知自回归解码的连续扩散，Continuous Diffusion with Contextual AutoRegressive Decoder），一种两阶段框架：在整个嵌入空间中保持扩散过程完全连续，同时学习一个强大且上下文相关的离散化模块——一个自回归Transformer解码器，该解码器对去噪嵌入序列进行交叉注意力，并执行上下文化的词元舍入。在LM1B和OpenWebText数据集上的实验表明，CoDAR显著提升了生成质量，超越了潜在空间扩散模型，并在性能上与强大的离散DLMs相媲美，同时提供了一个简单的解码器温度控制旋钮，用于在流畅性与多样性之间进行权衡。

title: ParEVO: Synthesizing Code for Irregular Data: High-Performance Parallelism through Agentic Evolution
authors: Liu Yang, Zeyu Nie, Andrew Liu, Felix Zou, Deniz Altinbüken, Amir Yazdanbakhsh, Quanquan C. Liu
abstract: The transition from sequential to parallel computing is essential for modern high-performance applications but is hindered by the steep learning curve of concurrent programming. This challenge is magnified for irregular data structures (such as sparse graphs, unbalanced trees, and non-uniform meshes) where static scheduling fails and data dependencies are unpredictable. Current Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail catastrophically on these tasks, generating code plagued by subtle race conditions, deadlocks, and sub-optimal scaling. We bridge this gap with ParEVO, a framework designed to synthesize high-performance parallel algorithms for irregular data. Our contributions include: (1) The Parlay-Instruct Corpus, a curated dataset of 13,820 tasks synthesized via a "Critic-Refine" pipeline that explicitly filters for empirically performant algorithms that effectively utilize Work-Span parallel primitives; (2) specialized DeepSeek, Qwen, and Gemini models fine-tuned to align probabilistic generation with the rigorous semantics of the ParlayLib library; and (3) an Evolutionary Coding Agent (ECA) that improves the "last mile" of correctness by iteratively repairing code using feedback from compilers, dynamic race detectors, and performance profilers. On the ParEval benchmark, ParEVO achieves an average 106x speedup (with a maximum of 1103x) across the suite, and a robust 13.6x speedup specifically on complex irregular graph problems, outperforming state-of-the-art commercial models. Furthermore, our evolutionary approach matches state-of-the-art expert human baselines, achieving up to a 4.1x speedup on specific highly-irregular kernels. Source code and datasets are available at https://github.com/WildAlg/ParEVO.
title_zh: ParEVO：用于不规则数据的代码合成：通过代理进化实现高性能并行
abstract_zh: 从顺序计算向并行计算的转变对于现代高性能应用至关重要，但这一过程受到并发编程陡峭学习曲线的阻碍。这一挑战在不规则数据结构（如稀疏图、非平衡树和非均匀网格）中尤为突出，因为静态调度在此类场景下失效，数据依赖关系也难以预测。当前的大语言模型（LLMs）在这些任务上常常表现灾难性失败，生成的代码普遍存在微妙的竞态条件、死锁以及次优的可扩展性问题。为此，我们提出了ParEVO框架，旨在为不规则数据结构自动生成高性能的并行算法。我们的主要贡献包括：（1）Parlay-Instruct语料库，一个通过“批评-优化”流程生成的13,820个任务的精选数据集，该数据集明确筛选出在实际运行中表现优异、并有效利用Work-Span并行原语的算法；（2）针对ParlayLib库严格语义进行微调的专用DeepSeek、Qwen和Gemini模型，使其概率生成更符合并行编程规范；（3）一种进化式编码代理（ECA），通过编译器、动态竞态检测器和性能分析器的反馈，迭代修复代码，从而解决“最后一公里”的正确性问题。在ParEval基准测试中，ParEVO在整体任务集上实现了平均106倍（最高达1103倍）的加速比，尤其在复杂的不规则图问题上实现了稳健的13.6倍加速，显著优于当前最先进的商业模型。此外，我们的进化方法达到了与顶尖专家人类水平相当的性能，在某些高度不规则的核函数上实现了最高4.1倍的加速。源代码与数据集已公开，访问地址为：https://github.com/WildAlg/ParEVO。

title: NeuroProlog: Multi-Task Fine-Tuning for Neurosymbolic Mathematical Reasoning via the Cocktail Effect
authors: Pratibha Zunjare, Michael Hsiao
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on natural language tasks but remain unreliable in mathematical reasoning, frequently generating fluent yet logically inconsistent solutions. We present \textbf{NeuroProlog}, a neurosymbolic framework that ensures verifiable reasoning by compiling math word problems into executable Prolog programs with formal verification guarantees. We propose a multi-task Cocktail training strategy that jointly optimizes three synergistic objectives in a unified symbolic representation space: (i) mathematical formula-to-rule translation (KB), (ii) natural language-to-program synthesis (SOLVE), and (iii) program-answer alignment. This joint supervision enables positive transfer, where symbolic grounding in formula translation directly improves compositional reasoning capabilities. At inference, we introduce an execution-guided decoding pipeline with fine-grained error taxonomy that enables iterative program repair and quantifies model self-debugging capacity. Comprehensive evaluation on GSM8K across four model scales (3B--32B parameters) demonstrates consistent improvements: cocktail training achieves significant accuracy gains of +5.23\% (Qwen-32B, $p < 0.01$), +3.43\% (GPT-OSS-20B, $p < 0.01$), and +5.54\% (Llama-3B, $p < 0.05$) over single-task baselines.Systematic error analysis reveals scale-dependent learning dynamics: at 32B scale, cocktail training transforms unfixable type errors (12\% repair rate) into correctable domain errors (96\% repair rate), achieving 92.7\% overall correction; at 8B scale, the same training eliminates syntactic errors but introduces semantic failures, revealing a critical capacity threshold for type-safe symbolic reasoning.
title_zh: NeuroProlog：通过鸡尾酒效应实现神经符号数学推理的多任务微调
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在自然语言任务上表现出色，但在数学推理方面仍不可靠，常常生成看似流畅却逻辑不一致的解答。我们提出了**NeuroProlog**——一种神经符号框架，通过将数学应用题编译为具有形式化验证保障的可执行Prolog程序，确保推理过程的可验证性。我们设计了一种多任务“鸡尾酒”（Cocktail）训练策略，在统一的符号表示空间中联合优化三个协同的目标：(i) 数学公式到规则的翻译（KB），(ii) 自然语言到程序的合成（SOLVE），以及(iii) 程序与答案的一致性对齐。这种联合监督机制实现了正向迁移：公式翻译中的符号化基础直接提升了模型的组合推理能力。在推理阶段，我们引入了一种执行引导的解码流程，并结合细粒度错误分类体系，支持程序的迭代修复，并量化模型的自我调试能力。在GSM8K数据集上，针对四个模型规模（3B–32B参数）的全面评估表明，该方法持续提升性能：与单任务基线相比，“鸡尾酒”训练分别带来显著的准确率提升——Qwen-32B提升+5.23%（$p < 0.01$）、GPT-OSS-20B提升+3.43%（$p < 0.01$）、Llama-3B提升+5.54%（$p < 0.05$）。系统性错误分析揭示了规模依赖的学习动态：在32B规模下，“鸡尾酒”训练将原本无法修复的类型错误（修复率仅12%）转变为可修复的领域错误（修复率高达96%），整体修正率达到92.7%；而在8B规模下，相同训练虽能消除语法错误，却引入了语义错误，揭示出实现类型安全符号推理的关键能力阈值。

title: AI-for-Science Low-code Platform with Bayesian Adversarial Multi-Agent Framework
authors: Zihang Zeng, Jiaquan Zhang, Pengze Li, Yuan Qi, Xi Chen
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potentials for automating scientific code generation but face challenges in reliability, error propagation in multi-agent workflows, and evaluation in domains with ill-defined success metrics. We present a Bayesian adversarial multi-agent framework specifically designed for AI for Science (AI4S) tasks in the form of a Low-code Platform (LCP). Three LLM-based agents are coordinated under the Bayesian framework: a Task Manager that structures user inputs into actionable plans and adaptive test cases, a Code Generator that produces candidate solutions, and an Evaluator providing comprehensive feedback. The framework employs an adversarial loop where the Task Manager iteratively refines test cases to challenge the Code Generator, while prompt distributions are dynamically updated using Bayesian principles by integrating code quality metrics: functional correctness, structural alignment, and static analysis. This co-optimization of tests and code reduces dependence on LLM reliability and addresses evaluation uncertainty inherent to scientific tasks. LCP also streamlines human-AI collaboration by translating non-expert prompts into domain-specific requirements, bypassing the need for manual prompt engineering by practitioners without coding backgrounds. Benchmark evaluations demonstrate LCP's effectiveness in generating robust code while minimizing error propagation. The proposed platform is also tested on an Earth Science cross-disciplinary task and demonstrates strong reliability, outperforming competing models.
title_zh: 面向科学的AI低代码平台：基于贝叶斯对抗多智能体框架
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在自动化科学代码生成方面展现出巨大潜力，但其在可靠性、多智能体工作流中的错误传播问题，以及在成功指标定义不明确的领域中评估困难等方面仍面临挑战。为此，我们提出了一种专为科学领域人工智能（AI for Science, AI4S）任务设计的贝叶斯对抗式多智能体框架，以低代码平台（Low-code Platform, LCP）的形式实现。该框架协调三个基于LLM的智能体：任务管理器负责将用户输入结构化为可执行计划与自适应测试用例；代码生成器负责生成候选解决方案；评估器则提供全面的反馈。框架采用对抗性循环机制，任务管理器通过迭代优化测试用例来持续挑战代码生成器，同时利用贝叶斯原理动态更新提示分布，整合代码质量指标——功能正确性、结构一致性与静态分析结果。这种测试与代码的协同优化显著降低了对LLM可靠性的依赖，并有效缓解了科学任务中固有的评估不确定性。此外，LCP通过将非专家的自然语言提示自动转化为领域特定需求，大幅简化了人机协作流程，使无编程背景的实践者无需手动进行提示工程即可高效参与。基准测试结果表明，LCP在生成鲁棒性代码的同时，有效抑制了错误传播。该平台还在地球科学跨学科任务中进行了实测，表现出优异的可靠性，性能优于现有竞争模型。

title: Graph-GRPO: Stabilizing Multi-Agent Topology Learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization
authors: Yueyang Cang, Xiaoteng Zhang, Erlu Zhao, Zehua Ji, Yuhang Liu, Yuchen He, Zhiyuan Ning, Chen Yijun, Wenge Que, Li Shi
abstract: Optimizing communication topology is fundamental to the efficiency and effectiveness of Large Language Model (LLM)-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). While recent approaches utilize reinforcement learning to dynamically construct task-specific graphs, they typically rely on single-sample policy gradients with absolute rewards (e.g., binary correctness). This paradigm suffers from severe gradient variance and the credit assignment problem: simple queries yield non-informative positive rewards for suboptimal structures, while difficult queries often result in failures that provide no learning signal. To address these challenges, we propose Graph-GRPO, a novel topology optimization framework that integrates Group Relative Policy Optimization. Instead of evaluating a single topology in isolation, Graph-GRPO samples a group of diverse communication graphs for each query and computes the advantage of specific edges based on their relative performance within the group. By normalizing rewards across the sampled group, our method effectively mitigates the noise derived from task difficulty variance and enables fine-grained credit assignment. Extensive experiments on reasoning and code generation benchmarks demonstrate that Graph-GRPO significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving superior training stability and identifying critical communication pathways previously obscured by reward noise.
title_zh: Graph-GRPO：通过组相对策略优化稳定多智能体拓扑学习
abstract_zh: 优化通信拓扑结构是提升基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体系统（MAS）效率与效能的关键。尽管近期方法采用强化学习动态构建任务特定的图结构，但通常依赖于单样本策略梯度与绝对奖励（如二元正确性）的范式。这一方法存在严重的梯度方差问题以及信用分配难题：对于简单任务，次优的拓扑结构可能获得无信息量的正向奖励；而对于复杂任务，系统常因失败而无法提供任何学习信号。为解决上述挑战，我们提出 Graph-GRPO——一种新颖的拓扑优化框架，融合了分组相对策略优化（Group Relative Policy Optimization）。与以往仅孤立评估单一拓扑结构不同，Graph-GRPO 针对每个查询采样一组多样化的通信图，并基于各边在该组内的相对表现来计算其优势值。通过在采样组内对奖励进行归一化处理，我们的方法有效缓解了因任务难度差异带来的噪声干扰，实现了更精细的信用分配。在推理与代码生成基准上的大量实验表明，Graph-GRPO 显著优于现有最先进基线方法，不仅展现出更优的训练稳定性，还成功识别出此前被奖励噪声掩盖的关键通信路径。

title: SorryDB: Can AI Provers Complete Real-World Lean Theorems?
authors: Austin Letson, Leopoldo Sarra, Auguste Poiroux, Oliver Dressler, Paul Lezeau, Dhyan Aranha, Frederick Pu, Aaron Hill, Miguel Corredera Hidalgo, Julian Berman, George Tsoukalas, Lenny Taelman
abstract: We present SorryDB, a dynamically-updating benchmark of open Lean tasks drawn from 78 real world formalization projects on GitHub. Unlike existing static benchmarks, often composed of competition problems, hillclimbing the SorryDB benchmark will yield tools that are aligned to the community needs, more usable by mathematicians, and more capable of understanding complex dependencies. Moreover, by providing a continuously updated stream of tasks, SorryDB mitigates test-set contamination and offers a robust metric for an agent's ability to contribute to novel formal mathematics projects. We evaluate a collection of approaches, including generalist large language models, agentic approaches, and specialized symbolic provers, over a selected snapshot of 1000 tasks from SorryDB. We show that current approaches are complementary: even though an agentic approach based on Gemini Flash is the most performant, it is not strictly better than other off-the-shelf large-language models, specialized provers, or even a curated list of Lean tactics.
title_zh: 抱歉，DB：AI证明者能完成现实世界中的Lean定理吗？
abstract_zh: 我们提出了SorryDB，这是一个动态更新的开放Lean任务基准，其任务均来自GitHub上78个真实的正式化项目。与现有静态基准不同，后者通常由竞赛题目构成，而SorryDB的持续优化将催生更契合社区实际需求、对数学家更具实用价值、并能更好理解复杂依赖关系的工具。此外，通过提供持续更新的任务流，SorryDB有效缓解了测试集污染问题，并为评估智能体在新型正式数学项目中贡献能力提供了稳健的指标。我们在SorryDB中选取的1000个任务快照上，评估了一系列方法，包括通用型大语言模型、代理式方法以及专用符号证明器。结果表明，当前各类方法具有互补性：尽管基于Gemini Flash的代理式方法表现最佳，但其并未在所有情况下全面优于其他现成的大语言模型、专用证明器，甚至优于经过精心筛选的Lean策略列表。

title: MASPOB: Bandit-Based Prompt Optimization for Multi-Agent Systems with Graph Neural Networks
authors: Zhi Hong, Qian Zhang, Jiahang Sun, Zhiwei Shang, Mingze Kong, Xiangyi Wang, Yao Shu, Zhongxiang Dai
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved great success in many real-world applications, especially the one serving as the cognitive backbone of Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) to orchestrate complex workflows in practice. Since many deployment scenarios preclude MAS workflow modifications and its performance is highly sensitive to the input prompts, prompt optimization emerges as a more natural approach to improve its performance. However, real-world prompt optimization for MAS is impeded by three key challenges: (1) the need of sample efficiency due to prohibitive evaluation costs, (2) topology-induced coupling among prompts, and (3) the combinatorial explosion of the search space. To address these challenges, we introduce MASPOB (Multi-Agent System Prompt Optimization via Bandits), a novel sample-efficient framework based on bandits. By leveraging Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) to quantify uncertainty, the bandit framework balances exploration and exploitation, maximizing gains within a strictly limited budget. To handle topology-induced coupling, MASPOB integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to capture structural priors, learning topology-aware representations of prompt semantics. Furthermore, it employs coordinate ascent to decompose the optimization into univariate sub-problems, reducing search complexity from exponential to linear. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks demonstrate that MASPOB achieves state-of-the-art performance, consistently outperforming existing baselines.
title_zh: MASPOB：基于欺骗者提示优化的多智能体系统图神经网络方法
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在众多实际应用中取得了巨大成功，尤其是在作为多智能体系统（MAS）的认知核心，用于实际中协调复杂工作流方面表现突出。由于许多部署场景无法修改MAS的工作流，且其性能对输入提示（prompt）高度敏感，因此通过提示优化来提升性能成为一种更为自然的解决方案。然而，现实世界中的MAS提示优化面临三大关键挑战：（1）由于评估成本极高，需要具备样本高效性；（2）提示之间存在由系统拓扑结构引起的耦合关系；（3）搜索空间存在组合爆炸问题。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了MASPOB（基于多臂赌博机的多智能体系统提示优化），这是一种基于赌博机的新型样本高效优化框架。通过利用上置信界（UCB）来量化不确定性，该赌博机框架在探索与利用之间实现平衡，在严格受限的预算下最大化收益。为处理拓扑引起的耦合问题，MASPOB引入图神经网络（GNN）以捕捉结构先验信息，学习具有拓扑感知能力的提示语义表示。此外，该方法采用坐标上升法将优化问题分解为单变量子问题，将搜索复杂度从指数级降低至线性级。在多个不同基准上的大量实验表明，MASPOB实现了当前最优的性能，持续优于现有各类基线方法。

title: Extending the Formalism and Theoretical Foundations of Cryptography to AI
authors: Federico Villa, F. Betül Durak, Tadayoshi Kohno, Tapdig Maharramli, Franziska Roesner
abstract: Recent progress in (Large) Language Models (LMs) has enabled the development of autonomous LM-based agents capable of executing complex tasks with minimal supervision. These agents have started to be integrated into systems with significant autonomy and authority. The security community has been studying their security. One emerging direction to mitigate security risks is to constrain agent behaviours via access control and permissioning mechanisms. Existing permissioning proposals, however, remain difficult to compare due to the absence of a shared formal foundation. This work provides such a foundation. We first systematize the landscape by constructing an attack taxonomy tailored to language models, the computational primitives of agentic systems. We then develop a formal treatment of agentic access control by defining an AIOracle algorithmically and introducing a security-game framework that captures completeness (in the absence of an adversary) and adversarial robustness. Our security game unifies confidentiality, integrity, and availability within a single model. Using this framework, we show that existing approaches to confidentiality of training data fundamentally conflict with completeness. Finally, we formalize a modular decomposition of helpfulness and harmlessness objectives and prove its soundness, in order to enable principled reasoning about the security of agentic system designs. Our studies suggests that if we were to design a secure system with measurable security, then we might want to use a modular approach to break the problem into sub-problems and let the composition on different modules complete the design. Our studies show that this natural approach with the relevant formalism is needed to prove security reductions.
title_zh: 将密码学的形式化与理论基础扩展至人工智能
abstract_zh: 近年来，大型语言模型（LMs）的进展推动了自主型基于语言模型的智能体的发展，这些智能体能够在极少监督的情况下执行复杂任务。这些智能体正逐步被集成到具有高度自主性和权威性的系统中。安全领域已开始关注其潜在的安全风险。一个新兴的缓解策略是通过访问控制和权限机制来约束智能体的行为。然而，现有的权限设计提案由于缺乏统一的正式基础，难以进行有效比较。本文为此提供了这样的基础。我们首先通过构建一个针对语言模型（即智能体系统计算原语）的攻击分类法，系统化地梳理了当前的研究格局。随后，我们通过算法化定义一个AIOracle，提出了一种形式化的智能体访问控制框架，并引入了一个安全博弈模型，该模型能够同时刻画完备性（在无攻击者情况下）和对抗鲁棒性。我们的安全博弈模型将机密性、完整性与可用性统一在一个框架内。基于该框架，我们证明了现有训练数据机密性保护方法与系统完备性之间存在根本性冲突。最后，我们形式化地提出了“有益性”与“无害性”目标的模块化分解，并证明了其合理性，从而为智能体系统设计的安全性提供严谨的推理基础。我们的研究表明，若要设计一个具备可度量安全性的系统，应采用模块化方法，将问题分解为若干子问题，再通过模块间的组合完成整体设计。我们的研究进一步表明，结合适当的正式化工具，这种自然的模块化方法是实现安全归约所必需的。

title: Human-Certified Module Repositories for the AI Age
authors: Szilárd Enyedi
abstract: Human-Certified Module Repositories (HCMRs) are introduced in this work as a new architectural model for constructing trustworthy software in the era of AI-assisted development. As large language models increasingly participate in code generation, configuration synthesis, and multi-component integration, the reliability of AI-assembled systems will depend critically on the trustworthiness of the building blocks they use. Today's software supply-chain incidents and modular development ecosystems highlight the risks of relying on components with unclear provenance, insufficient review, or unpredictable composition behavior. We argue that future AI-driven development workflows require repositories of reusable modules that are curated, security-reviewed, provenance-rich, and equipped with explicit interface contracts. To this end, we propose HCMRs, a framework that blends human oversight with automated analysis to certify modules and support safe, predictable assembly by both humans and AI agents. We present a reference architecture for HCMRs, outline a certification and provenance workflow, analyze threat surfaces relevant to modular ecosystems, and extract lessons from recent failures. We further discuss implications for governance, scalability, and AI accountability, positioning HCMRs as a foundational substrate for reliable and auditable AI-constructed software systems.
title_zh: 人类认证的模块仓库：人工智能时代的新篇章
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种名为“人类认证模块仓库”（Human-Certified Module Repositories, HCMRs）的新架构模型，旨在应对人工智能辅助开发时代构建可信软件的挑战。随着大型语言模型在代码生成、配置合成及多组件集成中的深度参与，AI组装系统的可靠性将越来越依赖于其所使用构建模块的可信性。当前软件供应链事件以及模块化开发生态系统的现状凸显了依赖来源不明、审查不足或行为不可预测组件所带来的风险。我们认为，未来的AI驱动开发流程需要建立可复用的模块仓库，这些仓库中的模块应经过精心筛选、安全审查、具备丰富的来源信息，并配有明确的接口契约。为此，我们提出了HCMRs框架，通过融合人工监督与自动化分析，对模块进行认证，从而支持人类与AI代理安全、可预测地组装软件。本文提出了HCMRs的参考架构，阐述了认证与来源追溯的工作流程，分析了模块化生态系统中的相关威胁面，并从近期失败案例中提炼出重要经验教训。此外，我们还探讨了治理、可扩展性以及AI责任等议题，将HCMRs定位为构建可靠、可审计的AI生成软件系统的基础性支撑。

title: Dynamic Contract Analysis for Parallel Programming Models
authors: Yussur Mustafa Oraji, Alexander Hück, Christian Bischof
abstract: Parallel programming in high-performance computing depends on low-level APIs such as MPI, requiring users to manage synchronization and resources manually. Several correctness checking tools exist to help bug-free code development, though most target a single programming model, limiting their applicability. Our previous work, the static analysis tool CoVer, leverages a contract-based approach enabling users to specify custom error-checking rules and support emerging or unconventional programming models without requiring extensive new tooling. However, static analysis cannot fully reason about runtime-dependent behavior such as pointer aliasing or indirect control flow. To address this, we present CoVer-Dynamic, a dynamic analysis extension that reuses CoVer's contract language to provide a unified static-dynamic verification framework. By enforcing the same contracts at runtime, CoVer-Dynamic improves classification accuracy and eliminates false positives on standardized MPI and OpenSHMEM benchmarks, while detecting errors beyond static analysis only. Our evaluation shows that CoVer-Dynamic consistently outperforms the state-of-the-art correctness checker MUST, averaging a 2x speedup. Finally, our results show limitations in the expressiveness of the contract language, motivating future work to support more error classes.
title_zh: 并行编程模型的动态合同分析
abstract_zh: 高性能计算中的并行编程依赖于MPI等低级API，要求用户手动管理同步和资源。尽管已存在一些正确性检查工具以帮助开发无错误的代码，但大多数工具仅针对单一编程模型，限制了其适用范围。我们之前的工作——静态分析工具CoVer，采用基于契约的方法，使用户能够指定自定义的错误检查规则，并支持新兴或非传统的编程模型，而无需开发大量新工具。然而，静态分析无法完全推理依赖运行时行为的特性，例如指针别名或间接控制流。为解决这一问题，我们提出了CoVer-Dynamic，这是一个动态分析扩展，复用了CoVer的契约语言，构建了一个统一的静态-动态验证框架。通过在运行时强制执行相同的契约，CoVer-Dynamic显著提升了分类准确率，并在标准的MPI和OpenSHMEM基准测试中消除了静态分析带来的误报，同时能够检测出静态分析无法发现的错误。我们的评估表明，CoVer-Dynamic在性能上始终优于当前最先进的正确性检查工具MUST，平均提速达2倍。最后，我们的研究也揭示了契约语言表达能力的局限性，这为未来工作支持更多错误类型提供了方向。

title: Causal Learning Should Embrace the Wisdom of the Crowd
authors: Ryan Feng Lin, Yuantao Wei, Huiling Liao, Xiaoning Qian, Shuai Huang
abstract: Learning causal structures typically represented by directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) from observational data is notoriously challenging due to the combinatorial explosion of possible graphs and inherent ambiguities in observations. This paper argues that causal learning is now ready for the emergence of a new paradigm supported by rapidly advancing technologies, fulfilling the long-standing vision of leveraging human causal knowledge. This paradigm integrates scalable crowdsourcing platforms for data collection, interactive knowledge elicitation for expert opinion modeling, robust aggregation techniques for expert reconciliation, and large language model (LLM)-based simulation for augmenting AI-driven information acquisition. In this paper, we focus on DAG learning for causal discovery and frame the problem as a distributed decision-making task, recognizing that each participant (human expert or LLM agent) possesses fragmented and imperfect knowledge about different subsets of the variables of interest in the causal graph. By proposing a systematic framework to synthesize these insights, we aim to enable the recovery of a global causal structure unachievable by any individual agent alone.We advocate for a new research frontier and outline a comprehensive framework for new research thrusts that range from eliciting, modeling, aggregating, and optimizing human causal knowledge contributions.
title_zh: 因果学习应拥抱群体智慧
abstract_zh: 从观测数据中学习通常以有向无环图（DAG）表示的因果结构，由于可能图的组合爆炸以及观测数据固有的模糊性，一直是一项极具挑战性的任务。本文认为，随着技术的飞速发展，因果学习正迎来一个新范式的出现，这将实现长期以来利用人类因果知识的愿景。这一新范式整合了可扩展的众包平台用于数据收集、交互式知识获取用于专家意见建模、稳健的聚合技术用于专家意见的协调，以及基于大语言模型（LLM）的模拟技术，以增强人工智能驱动的信息获取能力。本文聚焦于因果发现中的DAG学习，将该问题视为一个分布式决策任务，认识到每位参与者（人类专家或LLM代理）仅对因果图中不同变量子集拥有片段化且不完善的认知。通过提出一个系统性的框架来综合这些见解，我们旨在实现单一智能体无法达成的全局因果结构的恢复。本文倡导开辟一个全新的研究前沿，并系统性地勾勒出一系列研究方向，涵盖人类因果知识的获取、建模、聚合与优化。

title: IMR-LLM: Industrial Multi-Robot Task Planning and Program Generation using Large Language Models
authors: Xiangyu Su, Juzhan Xu, Oliver van Kaick, Kai Xu, Ruizhen Hu
abstract: In modern industrial production, multiple robots often collaborate to complete complex manufacturing tasks. Large language models (LLMs), with their strong reasoning capabilities, have shown potential in coordinating robots for simple household and manipulation tasks. However, in industrial scenarios, stricter sequential constraints and more complex dependencies within tasks present new challenges for LLMs. To address this, we propose IMR-LLM, a novel LLM-driven Industrial Multi-Robot task planning and program generation framework. Specifically, we utilize LLMs to assist in constructing disjunctive graphs and employ deterministic solving methods to obtain a feasible and efficient high-level task plan. Based on this, we use a process tree to guide LLMs to generate executable low-level programs. Additionally, we create IMR-Bench, a challenging benchmark that encompasses multi-robot industrial tasks across three levels of complexity. Experimental results indicate that our method significantly surpasses existing methods across all evaluation metrics.
title_zh: IMR-LLM：基于大语言模型的工业多机器人任务规划与程序生成
abstract_zh: 在现代工业生产中，多个机器人通常协同工作以完成复杂的制造任务。大型语言模型（LLMs）凭借其强大的推理能力，在协调机器人完成简单家庭场景和操作任务方面已展现出巨大潜力。然而，在工业场景中，任务内部存在更严格的顺序约束和更复杂的依赖关系，这为LLMs带来了新的挑战。为此，我们提出了IMR-LLM——一种新型的由大型语言模型驱动的工业多机器人任务规划与程序生成框架。具体而言，我们利用LLMs辅助构建析取图，并采用确定性求解方法获得可行且高效的高层任务规划。在此基础上，我们引入过程树来引导LLMs生成可执行的低层程序。此外，我们构建了IMR-Bench这一具有挑战性的基准测试集，涵盖三个不同复杂度层级的多机器人工业任务。实验结果表明，我们的方法在所有评估指标上均显著优于现有方法。

title: StitchCUDA: An Automated Multi-Agents End-to-End GPU Programing Framework with Rubric-based Agentic Reinforcement Learning
authors: Shiyang Li, Zijian Zhang, Winson Chen, Yuebo Luo, Mingyi Hong, Caiwen Ding
abstract: Modern machine learning (ML) workloads increasingly rely on GPUs, yet achieving high end-to-end performance remains challenging due to dependencies on both GPU kernel efficiency and host-side settings. Although LLM-based methods show promise on automated GPU kernel generation, prior works mainly focus on single-kernel optimization and do not extend to end-to-end programs, hindering practical deployment. To address the challenge, in this work, we propose StitchCUDA, a multi-agent framework for end-to-end GPU program generation, with three specialized agents: a Planner to orchestrate whole system design, a Coder dedicated to implementing it step-by-step, and a Verifier for correctness check and performance profiling using Nsys/NCU. To fundamentally improve the Coder's ability in end-to-end GPU programming, StitchCUDA integrates rubric-based agentic reinforcement learning over two atomic skills, task-to-code generation and feedback-driven code optimization, with combined rubric reward and rule-based reward from real executions. Therefore, the Coder learns how to implement advanced CUDA programming techniques (e.g., custom kernel fusion, cublas epilogue), and we also effectively prevent Coder's reward hacking (e.g., just copy PyTorch code or hardcoding output) during benchmarking. Experiments on KernelBench show that StitchCUDA achieves nearly 100% success rate on end-to-end GPU programming tasks, with 1.72x better speedup over the multi-agent baseline and 2.73x than the RL model baselines.
title_zh: StitchCUDA：基于评分标准的智能体强化学习自动化多智能体端到端GPU编程框架
abstract_zh: 现代机器学习（ML）工作负载越来越多地依赖于GPU，但由于同时受GPU内核效率和主机端设置的影响，实现高性能的端到端计算仍然面临挑战。尽管基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法在自动化GPU内核生成方面展现出潜力，但以往研究主要集中在单个内核的优化上，未能扩展到完整的端到端程序，限制了其实际部署能力。为应对这一挑战，本文提出StitchCUDA——一种面向端到端GPU程序生成的多智能体框架，包含三个专业智能体：规划器（Planner）负责统筹整个系统设计，编码器（Coder）逐步实现设计方案，验证器（Verifier）则利用Nsys/NCU进行正确性检查与性能分析。为从根本上提升编码器在端到端GPU编程中的能力，StitchCUDA引入了基于评分标准的智能体强化学习机制，针对两项基础技能——任务到代码生成和基于反馈的代码优化——进行训练，并结合来自真实执行的综合评分奖励与规则奖励。由此，编码器能够学习实现高级CUDA编程技术（如自定义内核融合、cuBLAS后处理等），同时在基准测试中有效防止了奖励滥用行为（如直接复制PyTorch代码或硬编码输出）。在KernelBench上的实验表明，StitchCUDA在端到端GPU编程任务中实现了接近100%的成功率，相比多智能体基线提升1.72倍加速比，相比强化学习模型基线提升2.73倍加速比。

title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhixia Zhang, Zixuan Huang, Xin Xia, Deqing Wang, Fuzhen Zhuang, Shuai Ma, Ning Ding, Yaodong Yang, Jianxin Li, Yikun Ban
abstract: We introduce Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning (HACRL), a new learning paradigm that addresses the inefficiencies of isolated on-policy optimization. HACRL enables collaborative optimization with independent execution: heterogeneous agents share verified rollouts during training to mutually improve, while operating independently at inference time. Unlike LLM-based multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), HACRL does not require coordinated deployment, and unlike on-/off-policy distillation, it enables bidirectional mutual learning among heterogeneous agents rather than one-directional teacher-to-student transfer. Building on this paradigm, we propose HACPO, a collaborative RL algorithm that enables principled rollout sharing to maximize sample utilization and cross-agent knowledge transfer. To mitigate capability discrepancies and policy distribution shifts, HACPO introduces four tailored mechanisms with theoretical guarantees on unbiased advantage estimation and optimization correctness. Extensive experiments across diverse heterogeneous model combinations and reasoning benchmarks show that HACPO consistently improves all participating agents, outperforming GSPO by an average of 3.3\% while using only half the rollout cost.
title_zh: 异构智能体协作强化学习
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新型学习范式——异构智能体协作强化学习（Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning, HACRL），以解决孤立的在线策略优化中存在的效率问题。HACRL实现了独立执行下的协作优化：在训练过程中，异构智能体共享经过验证的轨迹以相互提升，而在推理阶段则独立运行。与基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体强化学习（MARL）不同，HACRL无需协调部署；与在线/离线策略蒸馏方法不同，它支持异构智能体之间的双向互学，而非单向的“教师到学生”知识传递。基于这一范式，我们提出了HACPO——一种协作式强化学习算法，通过有原则的轨迹共享机制，最大化样本利用率并促进跨智能体的知识迁移。为缓解能力差异和策略分布偏移问题，HACPO引入了四种针对性机制，并在理论上保证了无偏优势估计与优化正确性。在多种异构模型组合及推理基准上的大量实验表明，HACPO能够持续提升所有参与智能体的性能，平均比GSPO提升3.3%，同时仅需一半的轨迹生成成本。

title: Beyond Task Completion: Revealing Corrupt Success in LLM Agents through Procedure-Aware Evaluation
authors: Hongliu Cao, Ilias Driouich, Eoin Thomas
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents are increasingly adopted in high-stakes settings, but current benchmarks evaluate mainly whether a task was completed, not how. We introduce Procedure-Aware Evaluation (PAE), a framework that formalizes agent procedures as structured observations and exposes consistency relationships between what agents observe, communicate, and execute. PAE evaluates agents along complementary axes (Utility, Efficiency, Interaction Quality, Procedural Integrity) and applies multi-dimensional gating that categorically disqualifies corrupt outcomes. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLM agents on tau-bench yields findings at the axis, compliance, and benchmark levels. At the axis level, the dimensions capture non-redundant failure modes: utility masks reliability gaps, speed does not imply precision, and conciseness does not predict intent adherence. At the procedural compliance level, 27-78% of benchmark reported successes are corrupt successes concealing violations across interaction and integrity. Furthermore, gating substantially collapses Pass^4 rate and affects model rankings. The analysis of corrupt success cases reveals distinctive per-model failure signatures: GPT-5 spreads errors across policy, execution, and intent dimensions; Kimi-K2-Thinking concentrates 78% of violations in policy faithfulness and compliance; and Mistral-Large-3 is dominated by faithfulness failures. At the benchmark level, our analysis exposes structural flaws in the benchmark design, including task scope gaps, contradictory reward signals, and simulator artifacts that produce accidental successes.
title_zh: 超越任务完成：通过程序感知评估揭示大语言模型智能体中的腐败成功
abstract_zh:
基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在高风险场景中的应用日益广泛，但当前的评估基准主要关注任务是否完成，而忽视了任务完成的方式。为此，我们提出了程序感知评估（Procedure-Aware Evaluation, PAE）框架，将智能体的执行过程形式化为结构化观测，并揭示智能体在观察、沟通与执行之间的一致性关系。PAE从多个互补维度对智能体进行评估：实用性（Utility）、效率（Efficiency）、交互质量（Interaction Quality）和程序完整性（Procedural Integrity），并引入多维门控机制，对存在缺陷的结果进行分类剔除。

在tau-bench基准上对当前最先进的LLM智能体进行评估，我们获得了从维度、合规性到基准整体层面的深入发现。在维度层面，各评估维度捕捉了非冗余的失败模式：实用性掩盖了可靠性缺陷，速度并不等同于精确性，简洁性也无法预测意图遵循程度。在程序合规层面，27%至78%的基准报告的成功结果实为“虚假成功”——这些结果隐藏了在交互行为与程序完整性方面的违规。此外，引入门控机制后，“Pass^4”通过率显著下降，且模型排名也发生明显变化。

对虚假成功案例的分析揭示了各模型独特的失败特征：GPT-5的错误广泛分布在策略、执行和意图三个维度；Kimi-K2-Thinking的78%违规集中于策略忠实性与合规性；而Mistral-Large-3则主要受忠实性失败主导。在基准整体层面，我们的分析暴露了基准设计中的结构性缺陷，包括任务范围不足、奖励信号相互矛盾，以及模拟器固有缺陷导致的偶然成功。

这些发现表明，仅以任务完成率作为评估标准存在严重局限，亟需引入如PAE这样的程序感知评估框架，以更全面、严谨地衡量智能体的真实能力与可靠性。

title: Step-Level Sparse Autoencoder for Reasoning Process Interpretation
